title: shebang fixes for rpm compilation
body:
Jindrich sent me an email with a number of soft errors from the rpm
building process that he does.  I've change things to make the errors go
away.

They are:
```
mangling shebang in /usr/share/buildah/test/system/digest/make-v2sN from /bin/bash to #!/usr/bin/bash
mangling shebang in /usr/share/buildah/test/system/test_buildah_authentication.sh from /usr/bin/env bash to #!/usr/bin/bash
mangling shebang in /usr/share/buildah/test/system/test_buildah_baseline.sh from /usr/bin/env bash to #!/usr/bin/bash
mangling shebang in /usr/share/buildah/test/system/test_buildah_build_rpm.sh from /usr/bin/env bash to #!/usr/bin/bash
mangling shebang in /usr/share/buildah/test/system/test_runner.sh from /usr/bin/env bash to #!/usr/bin/bash
mangling shebang in /usr/share/buildah/test/system/validate/git-validation.sh from /bin/bash to #!/usr/bin/bash
mangling shebang in /usr/share/buildah/test/system/validate/whitespace.sh from /bin/sh to #!/usr/bin/sh
```

I seem to recall that we purposefully add `/usr/bin/env bash` in a few of these prior, IDK if this change will upset the apple cart or not.

Signed-off-by: TomSweeneyRedHat <tsweeney@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *TomSweeneyRedHat* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fbuildah). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/buildah/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [TomSweeneyRedHat] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

`/usr/bin/bash` will piss off the Gentoo maintainer: that path doesn't exist. The Gentoo maintainer is a very nice person: s/he always, always has packages ready the day after a release. I really appreciate that.

I have strong opinions about `#!/bin/bash`. It has been a UNIX convention for a long, long time. It offends me that some distro has taken it upon themselves to break this. But this is not my battle.
I would like to dig deeper into why @jnovy is seeing soft errors.  And keep the way we have it to keep Gentoo happy

Ok, investigated this a bit further. Messages produced by RPM is not real warnings/errors. It just notifies in the log that it has mangled shebangs during the build to assure usual format via %{__brp_mangle_shebangs} macro post-build.

That means there is no need to fix anything upstream as RPM will automatically convert shebangs to OS supported format downstream. We can close this one @TomSweeneyRedHat . Apologies for confusion.
https://pagure.io/packaging-committee/issue/738 - more information about automatic shebang mangling here.
Thanks all for the reviews and further digging.  I'll happily close this out then.
